events.description: We had such a great time at the first Silent Listening Party that we had to bring it back! events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: James Hutton Turns 300!
events.description: Join us in the Crown Family PlayLab for a free, drop-in PlayDate celebrating the 300th Birthday of James Hutton, the Father of Modern Geology! Families with 2-6 year olds are invited to a free, drop-in, storytime session hosted by the Field Museum Geology Collections team to honor James Hutton, who established theories that impact our geology sciences today.

events.title: James Hutton, the father of Modern Geology, turns 300 years old
events.description: Learn about James Hutton and how angular unconformities teach us about deep time, and how modern processes going on now teach us about the rock cycle. Presenters include Paul Mayer, Fossil Invertebrate Collections Manager, and James Holstein, Geology Collections Manager

events.description: The DuPage County Fair July 23-26, 2026 The DuPage County Fair is one of Wheaton’s highlighted community events. We love seeing all community members come together to enjoy quality time with their neighbors, friends, and family. events.title: Poetry Pop-up: Poets in the Field
events.description: Imagine what it would feel like to stand next to a T. rex. Ponder what flowers smelled like 30 million years ago. See what inspiration you'll find and start turning your ideas into poetry with Eric Elshtain, the Field Museum's first poet in residence. Join us for a series of weekly events that change how you see the museum—whether you’ve visited

events.title: ¡Tocamos! Oaxacan Folk Art by Avelino Pérez
events.description: The Field Museum is proud to showcase new sculptures from the collection by Oaxacan artist Avelino Pérez. These beautifully carved and painted figures were created in collaboration with the bilingual children's book author, Cynthia Weill, for her latest publication ¡Tocamos! View our newest acquisitions from Oaxaca and learn about the Field Muse

events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Juneteenth Celebration
events.description: Families with children ages 2–6 are invited to a free, drop-in PlayDate to celebrate Juneteenth. This PlayDate will feature stories and a craft celebrating the liberation of enslaved Black Americans. Join us for a fun-filled PlayDate with stories and more!

events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Father's Day Storytime & Craft
events.description: Families with children ages 2–6 are invited to a free, drop-in PlayDate to celebrate fatherly figures in their lives on Father's Day. During this storytime and craft session, there will be stories about Dads and a coloring craft for children to share with their loved ones. Join us for a fun-filled PlayDate with stories, coloring, and more!

events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Pollinator Week Storytime with Chicago Public Library
events.description: Join us in the Crown Family PlayLab for a free, drop-in PlayDate outside in the Rice Native Gardens , celebrating Pollinator Week among the flowers and trees surrounding the Field Museum! events.title: Drawing Inspiration: Art in the Gardens
events.description: Everyone is invited to come observe and participate in Art in the Gardens! Field Museum staff and volunteers who dabble in the arts professionally or for fun will be scattered throughout Rice Native Gardens – the Museum’s largest and free outdoor exhibit, creating art inspired by the gardens in a variety of mediums. PlayLab PlayDate: Pollinator Week Storytime with the Field Museum Science Action Center
events.description: Families with 2-6 year olds are invited to a free, drop-in storytime session with guests Librarian Megan and Scientist Nicole that will leave you buzzing with excitement. This event will take place in the Rice Native Gardens (weather permitting) or in the Crown Family PlayLab in the case of inclement weather.

events.title: Nectar Feeders vs. Insect Hunters: A Closer Look at Bats
events.description: Celebrate Pollinator Week with scientists from the Gantz Family Collections Center! Bats are often celebrated as important nighttime pollinators, but not all bats play that role. At this special session, you’ll learn about the fascinating diversity of bat diets and adaptations around the globe. Even though Chicagoland’s bats do not pollinate our

events.title: Field Favorites in ASL
events.description: Field Favorites in ASL children family kids museum science art workshop

events.title: What Insects Call Our Garden Home (and Maybe Yours Too!)
events.description: Feel empowered to be a scientist! Check out equipment scientists use in the field to observe and document insects and see photos and specimens from the insect collections. Discover how even the smaller things in nature are important to biodiversity and learn ways you can document your own observations of nature. Aster Hasle, Lead Conservation Ecologist, and

Pinned, Preserved, and Powerful: How Collections Help Us Do Science
events.description: Educators, join us for Pollinator Week and explore how scientific collections support conservation, biodiversity research, and the process of doing science.
